facts.text: Newport became an incorporated village in 1857, and by 1860 it boasted 671 residents, a cotton factory employing 80 people, and a bank, gristmill, and three churches.
The James Keith House and the Brown‑Morey‑Davis Farm are the only two Newport structures listed on both the New York State Register of Historic Places and the National Register of Historic Places.
West Canada Creek cuts through the town, flowing along the eastern edge and helping to define that boundary.
The village of Newport sits on Route 28, right beside the eastern town line where West Canada Creek meets the road.
In 1805, Newport was carved out of parts of Fairfield, Herkimer, Norway, and Schuyler, forming a new town in Herkimer County.
The western border of Newport meets Oneida County, marking the town’s edge on the county line.
